sections.title: A: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

items.sentence: These days, most of us have a CD (1) ...... . Before the CD, (2) ...... made LPs, or 'long-playing' records. Although many (3) ...... have never seen an LP, they were once very popular. To play these records, you needed a record (4) ...... with a needle that ran along the record and produced the sound. Some (5) ...... say the sound of LPs was better than CDs – and many (6) ...... agree! LPs are no longer very popular as a form of (7) ...... , but many people buy and sell them. Some of them remember the LP from their (8) ...... and listening to records reminds them of the past.

sections.title: B: Complete using the verbs in the box
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the verbs in the box. You have to use one word twice.

items.sentence: Now, everyone knows this song, so I want you all to ...... in with me!
It's so noisy in this restaurant. Could you ask them to ...... the music down?
There was a fight during the match and the referee ...... two players off.
We ...... out about once a week and we cook at home the rest of the time.
I love this song! ...... it up!
I used to play the trumpet, but I ...... up last year because I didn't have time.
We stopped playing because of the rain, but when it stopped we ...... on.
A good way of getting more exercise is to ...... up a sport, like basketball.

sections.title: C: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
Jack really likes football and never misses a match. **crazy**
Jack ...... football and never misses a match.

My uncle worked on a sailing boat until he was thirty. **was**
My uncle ...... until he was thirty.

Do you want to watch TV? **feel**
Do you ...... TV?

John participated in a swimming competition last week. **part**
John ...... in a swimming competition last week.
